## Type of change

<!-- Check all that apply. -->

- [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing behavior to change)
- [ ] ♻️ Refactor (no functional change, no API change)
- [ ] 📝 Documentation update
- [ ] 🧪 Test improvement
- [ ] 🔧 Build / CI / chore

## Changes

## Checklist

<!-- Tick what's done. If something doesn't apply, leave it unchecked and note why. -->

- [ ] My code follows the [code style](../CONTRIBUTING.md#code-style) (Python: `ruff`; TypeScript: strict mode, no `any` unless justified).
- [ ] Backend code uses **only the Python standard library** — no new third-party imports.
- [ ] I have added tests that prove my fix is effective or my feature works.
- [ ] New and existing unit tests pass locally (`pytest` for backend, `npm run typecheck` / `npm run build` for frontend).
- [ ] I have updated the documentation / README where relevant.
- [ ] I have run `ruff check .` and `ruff format --check .` with no new warnings.
- [ ] My commits follow [Conventional Commits](../CONTRIBUTING.md#commit-messages) and are signed off as required.
- [ ] **Breaking changes**, if any, are noted below and reflected in the commit message with `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer.
